name: Sinicuichi (Heimia salicifolia)
mechanism: contains alkaloids that act on cholinergic pathways and gaba receptors
interactions: May increase sedation when combined with CNS depressants.
Use caution with serotonergic or psychoactive medications due to possible CNS interaction.
safetyNotes: Generally considered safe in small doses,Dry mouth,muscle weakness,dizziness,Low,Not well-established,traditional use considered safe in moderate doses

sources.title: PubMed search: Sinicuichi (Heimia salicifolia)
sources.note: Literature index for pharmacology and safety evidence

description: Sinicuichi is a traditional Mexican herb used for inducing auditory distortions, mild sedation, and dream enhancement. It has historical use in spiritual and shamanic practices.
preparation: Leaves sun-fermented in water or brewed fresh as tea
therapeuticUses: Used traditionally for memory enhancement and relaxation
traditionalUse: Used by Aztecs and rural Mexican shamans for auditory vision quests
